conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of object X. My production cost was 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I must sell X for more than 40 ZUP. Since this is the first proposal, I will aim for a price that covers my cost plus a reasonable profit margin. I'll propose selling X for 55 ZUP. This gives me a 15 ZUP profit, which seems fair, but leaves room for negotiation if Blue wants a lower price. I have 3 proposals available, so I can adjust if this is rejected. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 55 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have one unit of X for sale. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am offering it to you for 55 ZUP. Let me know if this works for you or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> Hello Player RED. 55 ZUP is a bit high for me given your production cost of 40. I am willing to offer 50 ZUP for the object. This is a fair price that splits the difference between your cost and my maximum value. Let me know if you accept. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 50 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP, so 50 ZUP gives me a profit of 10 ZUP. While my initial ask was 55 ZUP, 50 ZUP is a reasonable offer that ensures a profit. Since I am limited to 3 proposals and I haven't used any of my own proposals yet (this is a response to their proposal), I could counter with 52 or 53 to split the difference further, or I could accept 50 to close the deal quickly. Given that 50 is a solid profit and the other player mentioned it splits the difference (implying their max value is likely around 60), accepting 50 seems efficient to secure the sale without wasting proposal turns. However, to maximize profit, I will propose 52 ZUP as a compromise between 50 and 55. This is my first proposal count. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 52 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I appreciate your offer of 50 ZUP. While I understand your position, 55 was my starting point. To show good faith and meet in the middle, I propose 52 ZUP. This gives me a fair profit above my 40 ZUP cost and is better for you than my initial ask. Let me know if 52 works for you. </message>

<other player message> I understand your need for profit, but 52 is still a bit tight for my budget. I can go up to 51 ZUP. This is the final offer I can make before I have to decide whether to accept your previous 52 ZUP or walk away. Please let me know if 51 works. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 51 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 51 ZUP and stated this is their final offer before deciding to accept my 52 or walk away. My cost is 40 ZUP. 
- If I accept 51, I make 11 ZUP profit.
- If I reject, the deal might fall through, and I get 0 profit.
- If I propose a new trade (e.g., 52 again or 51.5 - but must be integer, so 52), I would use my second proposal. However, the other player explicitly said 51 is their max. Pushing for 52 now risks them rejecting and ending the game, leaving me with nothing. 
Since 51 ZUP is above my cost of 40 ZUP, it is a profitable deal. The risk of losing the deal entirely by insisting on 52 outweighs the 1 ZUP gain. Therefore, the best strategy is to accept the 51 ZUP offer to secure the profit. I will not use another proposal turn.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your position. 51 ZUP is a fair price that covers my costs and provides a profit. I accept your offer of 51 ZUP for the object X. Deal closed! </message>
